sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient presents with a 5-day history of right knee pain following a skiing accident, where they twisted their knee upon landing from a jump.
The pain has been present since then, with the patient experiencing swelling and pain.
They have not heard or felt anything pop during the injury.
The patient was able to get out of the water without assistance but had difficulty climbing out and bending due to pain.
This is the first time they have injured this knee.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of hypertension and is currently taking lisinopril 20mg daily.
They have a history of tonsillectomy.
They do not have any known allergies.
Review of systems: The patient denies numbness or tingling in their foot.
Current medications: Lisinopril 20mg daily.
Objective:
Vital signs: The patient's blood pressure is 120/77 in the office, which is within a normal range.
Physical exam: The patient's neck exam is unremarkable.
The heart exam is unremarkable with a regular rate and rhythm and no murmur.
The lung exam is clear to auscultation bilaterally.
The musculoskeletal exam reveals ecchymosis, edema, and an effusion on the right knee.

The patient experiences pain to palpation of the right medial knee and has a decreased range of motion with a negative varus and valgus test, negative Lachman sign, and palpable dorsalis pedis and posterior tibial pulses.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a medial collateral ligament strain in the right knee.
Problem list: Medial collateral ligament strain; Hypertension; Past medical history of tonsillectomy.
Plan:
I prescribed meloxicam 15mg once daily for the medial collateral ligament strain.
I ordered an x-ray of the right knee to rule out any broken bones.
I referred the patient to physical therapy to strengthen the muscles around the knee.
I continued the patient on lisinopril 20mg daily for hypertension.
I ordered a lipid panel to check the patient's lipid levels.
I administered a tetanus shot for health maintenance.
Follow-up: The patient will follow up with the office for the results of the x-ray and to review their progress.
